title: Sample preparation of label-free GAG disaccharides from biological tissues
author: H Davies-Strickleton

steps.title: 5 M Ammonium Acetate Stock, pH...
steps.instruction: 5 M Ammonium Acetate Stock, pH 7 Prepare around 50 mL, based on the following example: Weigh ~ 19.25 g Ammonium acetate VWR International (Avantor) Catalog #84885.180 Add the required volume of ddH 2 O using the formula below (for 19.25 g, add 50 mL ddH 2 O) Adjust to pH 7 by addition of a small volume of Ammonia solution 25% for HPL Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #5438300100 Exact weights and volumes can be adjusted using the formula below. Weigh approximately 19.25 g ammonium acetate, then use the formula below to calculate the volume of ddH 2 O to add (V) V = m / (C*MW) where: V = desired volume (L) m = mass of solute (g) C = desired concentration (M; mol/L) = 5 MW = molecular weight (g/mol) = 77.08 Example If 20 g ammonium acetate was weighed then the volume required would be: 20/(5*77.08) = 0.05189 L or 51.89 mL

steps.title: The following stocks, unless stated otherwise,...
steps.instruction: The following stocks, unless stated otherwise, can be prepared and stored at 4 o C for up to one year.

steps.title: 300 mM Calcium Chloride Stock Prepare...
steps.instruction: 300 mM Calcium Chloride Stock Prepare around 50 mL, based on the following example: Dissolve 1.665 g Calcium chloride Merck Millipore (EMD Millipore) Catalog #102391 (CAS: 10043-52-4) in 50 mL ddH 2 O Exact weights and volumes can be adjusted using the formula below. Weigh approximately 1.665 g CaCl 2 , then use the formula below to calculate the volume of ddH 2 O to add (V) V = m / (C*MW) where: V = desired volume (L) m = mass of solute (g) C = desired concentration (M) = 0.3 MW = molecular weight = 110.98 Example If 2g CaCl 2 was weighed then the volume required would be: 2/(0.3*110.98) = 0.0600 L or 60 mL

steps.title: 5 mM Magnesium Chloride Stock Prepare...
steps.instruction: 5 mM Magnesium Chloride Stock Prepare around 50 mL, based on the following example: Dissolve 50.8 mg Magnesium chloride hexahydrate Fischer Scientific Catalog #10647032 Add 50 mL ddH 2 O Exact weights and volumes can be adjusted using the formula below. Weigh approximately 50.8 mg (0.0508 g) MgCl 2 , then use the formula below to calculate the volume of ddH 2 O to add (V) V = m / (C*MW) where: V = desired volume (L) m = mass of solute (g) C = desired concentration (M) = 0.005 MW = molecular weight = 203.3 Example If 100 mg Mg Cl 2 was weighed then the volume required would be: 0.10/(0.005*203.3) = 0.09837 L or 98.37 mL

steps.title: 100 μM Calcium Chloride Solution Dilute...
steps.instruction: 100 μM Calcium Chloride Solution Dilute 300 mM Calcium Chloride Stock 1 in 3000. For example to make 15 mL: Add 15 mL ddH 2 O Add 5 µL 300 mM Calcium Chloride Stock go to step #3 Mix well Can be stored for 6 months at 4 o C

steps.title: 5 μM Magnesium Chloride Solution Dilute...
steps.instruction: 5 μM Magnesium Chloride Solution Dilute 5 mM Magnesium Chloride Stock 1 in 1000: Add 9.990 mL ddH 2 O Add 10 µL 5 mM Magnesium Chloride Stock go to step #4 Mix well Can be stored for 3 months at 4 o C

steps.title: 10 x Heparinase Buffer Composition of 10 x Heparinase Buffer
steps.instruction: 10 x Heparinase Buffer Composition of 10 x Heparinase Buffer: 400 mM Ammonium Acetate, pH 7 (1 in 12.5 dilution of the 5 M Ammonium Acetate stock) 30 mM Calcium Chloride ( 1 in 10 dilution of the 300 mM Calcium Chloride stock ) ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- The following formula was used to calculate the volume of each component required to prepare 10 x Heparinase Buffer: C_1 V_1 = C_2 V_2 rearranged provides: V_1 = \frac{C_2 V_2}{C_1} where: C1 = Starting concentration (mM) V1 = Volume of stock needed ( μL) C2 = Desired concentration (mM) V2 = Desired total volume (μL) e.g. The volume of 300 mM Calcium Chloride required to prepare 50 mL of 10x Heparinase Buffer with a final concentration of 30 mM is: V1 = (30*50000)/300 = 5000 μL ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Below, the preparation of 50 mL 10 x Heparinase Buffer is described. Should a higher volume be needed (e.g. to prepare 100 mL), use the formula above or simply multiply all component volumes accordingly (e.g. for total volume 100 mL, multiply all the volumes by 2). Example preparation of 50 mL 10x Heparinase Buffer: Add 41 mL ddH 2 O Add 4000 µL 5 M Ammonium Acetate Stock go to step #2 Add 5000 µL 300 mM Calcium Chloride Stock (1 in 10) go to step #3 Mix well Check pH, adjust to pH 7 if needed Can be stored for 6 months at 4 o C

steps.title: 10 x Chondroitinase Buffer Composition of...
steps.instruction: 10 x Chondroitinase Buffer Composition of 10 x Chondroitinase Buffer: 400 mM Ammonium Acetate, pH 7 (1 in 12.5 dilution of the 5 M Ammonium Acetate stock) 30 μM Calcium Chloride ( Note this is 1000 fold lower than the concentration in the 10x Heparinase Buffer; 1 in 10000 dilution of the 300 mM Calcium Chloride Stock. ) ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- The following formula was used to calculate the volume of each component required to prepare 10 x Chondroitinase Buffer: C_1 V_1 = C_2 V_2 rearranged provides: V_1 = \frac{C_2 V_2}{C_1} where: C1 = Starting concentration (mM) V1 = Volume of stock needed ( μL) C2 = Desired concentration (mM) V2 = Desired total volume (μL) e.g. The volume of 300 mM Calcium Chloride required to prepare 50 mL of 10x Chondroitinase Buffer with a final concentration of 30 μM is: V1 = (0.03*50000)/300 = 5 μL ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Below, the preparation of 50 mL 10 x Chondroitinase Buffer is described. Should a higher volume be needed (e.g. to prepare 100 mL), use the formula above or simply multiply all component volumes accordingly (e.g. for total volume 100 mL, multiply all the volumes by 2). Example preparation of 50 mL 10 x Chondroitinase Buffer: Add 46 mL ddH 2 O Add 4000 µL 5 M Ammonium Acetate Stock go to step #2 Add 5 µL 300 mM Calcium Chloride Stock (1 in 10000) go to step #3 Mix well Check pH, adjust to pH 7 if needed Can be stored for 6 months at 4 o C

steps.title: Pronase Buffer Composition of Pronase Buffer
steps.instruction: Pronase Buffer Composition of Pronase Buffer: 50 mM Ammonium Acetate pH 7 (1 in 10 dilution of the 500 mM Ammonium Acetate solution) 1 μM Calcium Chloride (1 in 100 dilution of the 100 μM Calcium Chloride Solution) Prepare fresh stock monthly. Ensure you have enough Pronase Buffer for all samples 0.2 mL is required per sample Therefore, 24 mL in the example below will be sufficient for > 100 samples For higher numbers of samples, multiply the volumes below accordingly (eg for 200 samples, multiply by a factor of 2) --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- The following formula was used to calculate the volume of each component required to prepare Pronase Buffer: C_1 V_1 = C_2 V_2 rearranged provides: V_1 = \frac{C_2 V_2}{C_1} where: C1 = Starting concentration (mM) V1 = Volume of stock needed ( μL) C2 = Desired concentration (mM) V2 = Desired total volume (μL) e.g. The volume of 100 μM Calcium Chloride required to prepare 24 mL of Pronase Buffer with a final concentration of 1 μM is: V1 = (0.001*24000)/0.1 = 240 μL Example preparation of 24 mL Pronase Buffer: Add 21.36 mL ddH 2 O Add 2.4 mL 500 mM Ammonium Acetate Stock pH 7 go to step #5 Add 240 µL 100 μM Calcium Chloride Solution go to step #6 Mix well Can be stored for 1 month at 4 o C

steps.title: Put all samples in 1
steps.instruction: Put all samples in 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes, label the tubes. In a preceeding protocol, diverse types of biological material can be disrupted . Here, this disrupted material is used to prepare label-free GAG disaccharides. All samples for testing are referred to as Test Samples. In addition to this, a Positive Control Sample (e.g. lung tissue homogenate) should be prepared alongside the Test Samples to be analysed. This serves to assess the consistency of this preparation of samples for GAG disaccharide analysis with previous analyses.

steps.title: Prepare fresh 1 mg/mL Pronase Stock Prepare immediately before use
steps.instruction: Prepare fresh 1 mg/mL Pronase Stock Prepare immediately before use. Open and handle Pronase inside a fume hood to prevent inhalation. Make a single Pronase Stock for all samples (plus 20% extra for spillage). ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Calculate the volume of Pronase Stock required Each sample requires 0.2 mL 1 mg/mL Pronase stock (this is deemed sufficient for the amount of samples outlined in the sample disruption protocol ). Calculate the volume of Pronase Stock required (including 20 % extra for spillage): V pronasestock (mL) = 1.2 * (V per sample (mL) * N ofsamples ) where: V per sample = 0.2 mL Example volume of Pronase Stock required for 50 samples: 1.2 x (0.2 mL x 50)= 12 mL Calculate the amount of Pronase to be weighed to give 1 mg/mL at the required volume Calculate the mass of Pronase required: Mass of Pronase (mg) = C * V where: C = 1 mg/mL V = calculated in the step above (here 12 mL) Example mass of Pronase required for 12 mL Pronase Stock (for 50 samples): Mass of Pronase (mg) = (1 * 12) = 12 mg

steps.title: Add 200 µL 1 mg/mL Pronase...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL 1 mg/mL Pronase Stock to each sample and mix briefly

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C overnight (mixing not required)
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C overnight (mixing not required)

steps.title: Vortex samples and continue incubation for 1 hour
steps.instruction: Vortex samples and continue incubation for 1 hour

steps.title: Heat deactivate at 98 °C for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Heat deactivate at 98 °C for 10 minutes

steps.title: Prepare 4 mg/mL DNase I Stock Prepare immediately before use
steps.instruction: Prepare 4 mg/mL DNase I Stock Prepare immediately before use. Open and handle DNase inside a fume hood to prevent inhalation. Make a single DNase I Stock for all samples (plus 20 % extra for spillage). ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Calculate the volume of DNase I Stock required Each sample requires 20 μL 4 mg/mL DNase stock (this is deemed sufficient for the amount of samples outlined in the sample disruption protocol ). Calculate the volume of DNase I Stock required (including 20 % extra for spillage): V dnasestock (mL) = 1.2 * (V per sample (mL) * N ofsamples ) where: V per sample = 0.02 mL Example volume of DNase I Stock required for 50 samples: 1.2 x (0.02 mL x 50) = 1.2 mL Calculate the amount of DNase I to be weighed to give 4 mg/mL at the required volume Calculate the mass of DNase I required: Mass of DNase I (mg) = C * V where: C = 4 mg/mL V = calculated in the step above (here 1.2 mL) Example mass of DNase I required for 1.2 mL DNase I Stock (for 50 samples): Mass of DNase I (mg) = (4 * 1.2) = 4.8 mg

steps.title: Add 20 µL 4 mg/mL DNase...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL 4 mg/mL DNase I stock to each sample

steps.title: Mix by vortexing briefly
steps.instruction: Mix by vortexing briefly

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 1...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 1 hour (mixing not required) Samples can be frozen here if needed.

steps.title: Thaw samples if required
steps.instruction: Thaw samples if required

steps.title: Pre-wash 500 μL 3 kDa MWCO...
steps.instruction: Pre-wash 500 μL 3 kDa MWCO filters with 200 µL ddH 2 O by centrifugation at 14000 x g, °C, for 10 minutes (discard flow through) Amicon Ultra-0.5 Centrifugal Filter Unit (Centrifugal Filter Unit, Millipore, UFC5003BK, https://www.merckmillipore.com/GB/en/product/Amicon-Ultra-0.5-Centrifugal-Filter-Unit,MM_NF-UFC5003BK )

steps.title: Load sample supernatants into pre-washed MWCO filters
steps.instruction: Load sample supernatants into pre-washed MWCO filters

steps.title: Centrifuge MWCO filters at 14000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge MWCO filters at 14000 x g, °C, for 25 minutes at ambient temperature, or until the retentate volume is 50-100 µL Discard the flow through

steps.title: Wash retentate with 400 µL ddH...
steps.instruction: Wash retentate with 400 µL ddH 2 O by centrifugation at 14000 x g, °C, for 25 minutes, or until 50-100 µL remains Discard the flow through

steps.title: Repeat the wash step and spin...
steps.instruction: Repeat the wash step and spin until the volume of the retentate is 20-50 µL

steps.title: Invert the MWCO filter and place...
steps.instruction: Invert the MWCO filter and place inside a fresh tube to collect the sample by centrifugation at 1000 x g, °C, for 2 minutes

steps.title: Pipette up/down and transfer samples to 1
steps.instruction: Pipette up/down and transfer samples to 1.5 mL protein lobind tubes Label the lids of the tubes (these will be re-used later)

steps.title: OPTIONAL
steps.instruction: OPTIONAL: If samples are to be analysed for both HS and CS/DS/HA disaccharides then divide the sample into two aliquots, one for HS analysis and for for CS/DS/HA analysis. The amount of sample to be used for HS and CS/DS/HA may vary (for instance some tissues have much higher CS/DS compared to HS). In the first instance divide the sample into two equal volumes: Use one half for HS preparation and the other half for CS/DS/HA preparation.

steps.title: Perform this section only for samples...
steps.instruction: Perform this section only for samples that require HS disaccharide analysis . Note: NEB enzymes are supplied with a 10 x Heparinase Buffer that have salts that are not compatible with downstream HILIC-MS/MS analysis. Therefore a different Heparinase Buffer is prepared and used here.

steps.title: Add 5 µL 10 x Heparinase...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL 10 x Heparinase Buffer to GAG enriched samples go to step #8 go to step #26

steps.title: Thaw the amount of each heparinase...
steps.instruction: Thaw the amount of each heparinase enzyme (Hep I, Hep II, Hep III) required for all samples Bacteroides Heparinase I - 600 units New England Biolabs Catalog #P0735L Bacteroides Heparinase II - 200 units New England Biolabs Catalog #P0736L Bacteroides Heparinase III - 35 units New England Biolabs Catalog #P0737L Each heparinase enzyme is stored at -80oC neat in 2-20 μL aliquots, only thaw the volume you need. Each sample requires 1 μL of each enzyme. Therefore for 12 samples, thaw 12 μL of each enzyme.

steps.title: Combine the Hep I, Hep II...
steps.instruction: Combine the Hep I, Hep II and Hep III into one tube = Heparinase Enzyme Mix. Spin briefly to collect at the bottom of the tube if needed. I.e. if there are 12 samples then combine 12 μL Hep I, 12 μL Hep II and 12 μL Hep III.

steps.title: Add 3 µL Heparinase Enzyme Mix to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 3 µL Heparinase Enzyme Mix to each sample

steps.title: Mix by pipetting or vortexing
steps.instruction: Mix by pipetting or vortexing. Spin briefly to collect at the bottom of the tube

steps.title: Incubate at 30 °C overnight, with mixing at 2000 rpm
steps.instruction: Incubate at 30 °C overnight, with mixing at 2000 rpm

steps.title: Perform this section only for samples...
steps.instruction: Perform this section only for samples that require CS/DS/HA disaccharide analysis .

steps.title: Add 5 µL 10 x Chondrotinase...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL 10 x Chondrotinase Buffer to GAG enriched samples go to step #9 go to step #26

steps.title: Thaw the amount of Chondrotinase ABC...
steps.instruction: Thaw the amount of Chondrotinase ABC (ChABC) enzyme required for all samples Chondroitinase ABC from Proteus vulgaris Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #C2905-10UN (CAS: 9024-13-9) ChABC is stored at -80oC in 10 μL aliquots at 10 mU/μL in 50 mM sodium acetate pH 7. Only thaw the volume you need. Each sample requires 1 μL of ChABC at 10 mU/μL (before dilution). Therefore for 12 samples, thaw 12 μL of 10 mU/μL ChABC.

steps.title: If there are multiple aliquots, combine them into one tube
steps.instruction: If there are multiple aliquots, combine them into one tube

steps.title: Dilute ChABC to 2
steps.instruction: Dilute ChABC to 2.5 mU/μL by adding 3 parts ddH 2 O This step allows a higher volume of enzyme to be pipetted into each sample, reducing pipetting error. For 12 samples, prepare 2.5 mU/μL as follows: Take 12 µL 10 mU/μL ChABC Add 36 µL ddH 2 O Mix well

steps.title: Add 4 µL 2
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL 2.5 mU/μL ChABC to each sample (Therefore the amount added is 10 mU/4 μL)

steps.title: Mix by pipetting or vortexing
steps.instruction: Mix by pipetting or vortexing. Spin briefly to collect at the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C overnight with mixing at 2000 rpm
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C overnight with mixing at 2000 rpm

steps.title: Prepare 1 μg/mL Internal Standard This...
steps.instruction: Prepare 1 μg/mL Internal Standard This section, and all subsequent sections, apply to both HS and CS/DS/HA disaccharide preparations. The Internal Standard is spiked into samples (and standards in a separate protocol ) and serves to to correct for variability in the extraction and analysis from this point. -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- The internal standard stock is stored at -20 o C in 10 µL aliquots of 1 mg/mL in ddH 2 O.

steps.title: Centrifuge all samples after GAG digestion...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge all samples after GAG digestion at 15000 rpm, °C, for 15 minutes at 4 °C

steps.title: Solid Phase Extraction (SPE) clean-up and...
steps.instruction: Solid Phase Extraction (SPE) clean-up and Internal Standard spike This steps acts as a scrub - disaccharides are hydrophilic and will not bind to the stationary phase of the HLB plate, whilst hydrophobic proteins and contaminants will bind and be removed. Oasis HLB 96-well µElution Plate, 2 mg Sorbent per Well, 30 µm, 1/pk (Sample preparation and filtration, Waters, 186001828BA)

steps.title: Over a waste collection plate, pre-wash...
steps.instruction: Over a waste collection plate, pre-wash the number of wells needed (one well per sample) with 100 µL ddH 2 O and centrifuge to waste at 1000 rpm, °C, for 2 minutes, or until wells are dry Discard the wash

steps.title: Transfer HLB plate on top of a clean collection plate
steps.instruction: Transfer HLB plate on top of a clean collection plate

steps.title: Assign a well to each sample %s %s
steps.instruction: Assign a well to each sample %s %s

steps.title: Load each sample supernatant into a HLB plate well
steps.instruction: Load each sample supernatant into a HLB plate well. go to step #30 Keep the lid of the microcentrifuge tubes for freeze-drying later. Carefully pierce several holes in the lid and remove the lid from the tube.

steps.title: Carefully use a multichannel pipette to...
steps.instruction: Carefully use a multichannel pipette to add 8 µL 1 μg/mL Internal Standard Solution to each well (ensure pipetting accuracy across all channels). go to step #29.3 Here 8 ng internal standard is added. At the end of the sample preparation the sample will be in 20 μL water, and so samples will have 8 ng/20 μL internal standard (400 ng/mL) When diluted 1in20 for HILIC-MS/MS analysis , this provides a concentration of 20 ng/mL

steps.title: Mix each well by pipetting up/down several times
steps.instruction: Mix each well by pipetting up/down several times

steps.title: Centrifuge at 100-500 x g, °C,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 100-500 x g, °C, for 3 minutes (aim for the sample to come through slowly to boost interaction with the solid phase of the well) Check the wells to see if the volume has come through If there is still volume repeat further spins at 500-2000 x g, °C, rpm for 3-5 minutes Or until all volume has come through the wells and into the collection plate

steps.title: Add 50 µL ddH 2 O...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL ddH 2 O to wells and centrifuge at 100-2000 x g, °C, rpm to collect with the sample flow through. Centrifuge until the wells are dry.

steps.title: For each sample transfer the collected...
steps.instruction: For each sample transfer the collected flow through to labelled 2 mL safelock tube. Add the lids with pierced holes in from earlier go to step #31.4 (The pierced lids enable lyophilisation) Leave the original lid still attached to the tube for later

steps.title: Freeze the samples at -80 °C...
steps.instruction: Freeze the samples at -80 °C or on dry ice briefly

steps.title: Freeze-dry the samples
steps.instruction: Freeze-dry the samples. Then cap them with the unpierced lid of the microtube.

steps.title: Store at 4 °C for subsequent vialling and HILIC-MS/MS analysis
steps.instruction: Store at 4 °C for subsequent vialling and HILIC-MS/MS analysis .

steps.title: 500 mM Ammonium Acetate Solution, pH...
steps.instruction: 500 mM Ammonium Acetate Solution, pH 7 Dilute 5 M Ammonium Acetate 1 in 10. For example to make 50 mL: Add 45 mL ddH 2 O Add 5 mL 5 M Ammonium Acetate Stock pH 7 go to step #2 Mix well, check pH, adjust to pH 7 if needed Can be stored for 6 months at 4 o C

steps.title: Thaw the 1 mg/mL Internal Standard...
steps.instruction: Thaw the 1 mg/mL Internal Standard Stock ∆UA-2S GlcNCOEt-6S (I-P) internal standard disaccharide Iduron Catalog #HD009

steps.title: Dilute the Internal Standard Stock to...
steps.instruction: Dilute the Internal Standard Stock to make a 10 μg/mL Internal Standard Solution (1 in 100 dilution): To the 10 µL aliquot add 990 µL ddH 2 O and mix well

steps.title: Dilute the 10 μg/mL Internal Standard...
steps.instruction: Dilute the 10 μg/mL Internal Standard Solution to make 1 μg/mL Internal Standard Solution (1 in 10 dilution): To a fresh microcentrifuge tube add 100 µL 10 μg/mL Internal Standard Solution Add 900 µL ddH 2 O Mix well Store at 4 °C when not in use

steps.title: Weigh the required amount of Pronase,...
steps.instruction: Weigh the required amount of Pronase, and prepare Pronase Stock: For example, for 50 samples prepare 12 mL 1 mg/mL Pronase Stock (see above for calculations): Weigh 12 mg Pronase Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #10165921001 Add 12 mL Pronase Buffer go to step #10 Mix well Exact weights and volumes can be adjusted using the formula below. Weigh approximately 12 mg Pronase, then use the formula below to calculate the volume of Pronase Buffer to add (V) V = m / C where: V = volume (mL) m = mass of solute (mg) C = concentration (mg/mL) = 1 Example If 14 mg Pronase was weighed then the volume required would be: 14/1 = 14 mL

steps.title: Weigh the required amount of DNase...
steps.instruction: Weigh the required amount of DNase I, and prepare DNase I Stock: For example for 50 samples prepare 1.2 mL 4 mg/mL DNase I Stock: Weigh 4.8 mg DNAse I Roche Catalog #10104159001 Add 1.2 mL 5 μM Magnesium Chloride Solution go to step #7 Mix well Exact weights and volumes can be adjusted using the formula below. Weigh approximately 5 mg DNase I, then use the formula below to calculate the volume of 5 μM Magnesium Chloride Solution to add (V) V = m / C where: V = volume (mL) m = mass of solute (mg) C = concentration (mg/mL) = 4 Example If 6 mg DNase I was weighed then the volume required would be: 6/4= 1.5 mL

steps.title: Centrifuge samples at 4 °C for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples at 4 °C for 10 minutes at 15000 rpm, °C,
